title: Convert hub from WebView to native RecyclerView
description:
Replace the WebView-based hub (index.html/script.js/style.css) with a native Kotlin Activity using RecyclerView. This is a prerequisite for all mini-apps since they need a native launcher.

Sub-tasks:
- Replace MainActivity WebView with a RecyclerView listing mini-apps
- Read app registry from a Kotlin data structure (no more apps-metadata.json in WebView)
- Each app entry shows name and description, tapping launches the Activity
- Keep the info page accessible (convert to native or keep as a secondary WebView)
- Remove webkit dependency
- Dark theme styling matching current design (#121212 background, light text)

This replaces the JS bridge approach (PARANOID-1t9, closed).
close_reason: Implemented: replaced WebView hub with native RecyclerView, AppRegistry, InfoActivity. Removed webkit dep. Committed as 3dd6df2.

title: SensorLogger: Service registers from session-frozen profile + filters write path (Phase A, ticket 2)
description:
Ticket 2 from update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph/tasks.md. Make SensorRecordingService consult the RecordingProfile for registration and the filtered write path, exposing the frozen snapshot for downstream consumers.

TDD ordering:
- 2.1 RED: ServiceRegistrationFromProfileTest (Robolectric) — profile enabling only accel + gyro → registerListener called exactly twice with expected sensors + rate constants.
- 2.2 RED: ServiceVisualizeOnlyFilterTest — magnetometer enabled=false visibleOnGraph=true → no writes, but live-stream pipeline sees the events.
- 2.3 RED: ServiceFrozenSnapshotTest — sessionProfile.value equals profile read at startRecording() even after RecordingProfileStore.update() mid-session.
- 2.4 GREEN: inject RecordingProfileStore into SensorRecordingService.
- 2.5 GREEN: on startRecording(), snapshot = store.flow.first(); sessionProfile.value = snapshot; register sensors where (enabled || visibleOnGraph) && rate != OFF AND device has sensor.
- 2.6 GREEN: expose sessionProfile: StateFlow<RecordingProfile?> on binder; clear to null on session end.
- 2.7 GREEN: in onSensorChanged, gate write-buffer append on sessionProfile.value?.get(type)?.enabled == true; always append to live ring buffer for registered sensors.
- 2.8 REFACTOR: extract pure shouldRegister(setting) predicate.

Spec: openspec/changes/update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph/tasks.md (Ticket 2)
Code: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/sensorlogger/service/SensorRecordingService.kt
acceptance_criteria: All Phase 2 tests pass; sensors with rateLevel=OFF never registered; mid-session profile updates do not affect frozen snapshot or in-flight registration.
notes:
SPEC DELTA: openspec/changes/update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph/specs/sensor-logger-recording/spec.md — Requirements MODIFIED 'Multi-sensor registration with FIFO batching', ADDED 'Session-frozen profile snapshot', ADDED 'Profile read failure falls back to defaults'.
CONTINUOUS VALIDATION (DEP-001): On completion, `openspec validate update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph --strict` MUST still pass. Run before marking this ticket done.
AMENDMENT (EXEC-003) — supersedes task 2.2:
2.2 RED (revised): ServiceVisualizeOnlyFilterTest — Given magnetometer in the session-frozen profile with enabled=false, visibleOnGraph=true, rateLevel=NORMAL, after delivering 10 simulated onSensorChanged calls for magnetometer:
  - the write buffer (drainable via test hook) is empty for magnetometer
  - sensorRegistered(TYPE_MAGNETIC_FIELD) returns true (verified via the fake SensorManager from 2.1)
  - no reference to the live-stream pipeline is needed; that path is exercised in PARANOID-imt (T3).
Phase A ticket 2 complete. Implemented pure policy helpers in service/RecordingPolicy.kt (shouldRegister, shouldWrite, SensorPresenceProbe, planRegistrations) + service/SensorManagerPresenceProbe.kt + config/RecordingProfileStoreFactory.kt (RecordingProfileStore.from(context) service-locator). Wired SensorRecordingService to snapshot the profile via runBlocking { store.flow.first() } on startRecording, expose sessionProfile: StateFlow<RecordingProfile?>, register sensors per planRegistrations, gate onSensorChanged write-append via shouldWrite, and clear snapshot to null on stop/disk-full. Tests: ServiceRegistrationFromProfileTest, ServiceVisualizeOnlyFilterTest, ServiceFrozenSnapshotTest — all pure JVM (Robolectric avoided per task-amendment + decomposition note). Validation: just test passes, just build passes, openspec --strict valid. Ro5u found one MEDIUM (runBlocking on main needs justification comment) — fixed.
close_reason: Phase A ticket 2 implemented per TDD with pure-helper decomposition; all tests + build + openspec pass.

title: SensorLogger: RecordingProfile domain model + DataStore persistence (Phase A, ticket 1)
description:
Ticket 1 from update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph/tasks.md. Introduce the per-sensor capture configuration as a pure-Kotlin domain model and a DataStore Preferences-backed store using flattened per-sensor primitive keys.

TDD ordering:
- 1.1 RED: `RecordingProfileDefaultTest` — asserts `RecordingProfile.Default` enables exactly accel + gyro + linear-accel at NORMAL with visibleOnGraph=true; all other sensors OFF/false/false.
- 1.2 RED: `RecordingProfileStoreTest` — round-trip preserves every per-sensor field for every `SensorType`, incl. a mixed-flags case (enabled=false, visibleOnGraph=true, rate=GAME).
- 1.3 RED: `RecordingProfileStoreFallbackTest` — IOException upstream → store emits `RecordingProfile.Default` without propagating.
- 1.4 GREEN: `SensorRateLevel` enum + `toSensorManagerDelay(): Int?`.
- 1.5 GREEN: `SensorCaptureSetting` + `RecordingProfile` + `Default`.
- 1.6 GREEN: `RecordingProfileStore` with flattened keys (`<name>_enabled`/`_rate`/`_visible`); reconstruct on read with per-sensor default fallback; write all keys atomically in one `edit { }`.
- 1.7 GREEN: `.catch { ... }` on the upstream `Flow<Preferences>` to satisfy 1.3.
- 1.8 GREEN: bookkeeping key `seen_capture_defaults_dialog_v2` + `hasSeenDefaultsDialog()` / `markDefaultsDialogSeen()`.
- 1.9 REFACTOR: extract `keysFor(sensor: SensorType)` helper.

Spec: openspec/changes/update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph/tasks.md (Ticket 1)
Code: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/sensorlogger/config/
acceptance_criteria: All Phase 1 unit tests pass; Default profile contents match the proposal.
notes:
FOLDED-IN (was T0 PARANOID-32w): Add `androidx.datastore:datastore-preferences:1.1.1` to `android/app/build.gradle.kts`. Verify with `./gradlew app:dependencies | grep datastore-preferences` inside Docker. Version pinned to match the latest stable known to ship with the current AGP.

DECOMPOSITION CHOICE (SCOPE-001): the 8-ticket set is decomposed horizontally (data → service → side-channel → UI) rather than as vertical tracer bullets. This is a deliberate choice because a single agent will implement the whole change serially; vertical reslicing would add coordination overhead without parallelism gain. See issue-review for the alternative slice plan if the choice ever needs to be revisited.

SPEC DELTA: openspec/changes/update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph/specs/sensor-logger-data/spec.md — Requirements 'Recording profile persistence', 'Profile read fallback on IO failure', 'First-launch dialog bookkeeping key'.
CONTINUOUS VALIDATION (DEP-001): On completion, `openspec validate update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph --strict` MUST still pass. Run before marking this ticket done.
Phase A ticket 1 complete. Implemented config/SensorRateLevel.kt (enum + toSensorManagerDelay), config/RecordingProfile.kt (SensorCaptureSetting + RecordingProfile.Default + OffSetting + get-with-fallback), config/RecordingProfileStore.kt (DataStore-backed; flattened <name>_{enabled,rate,visible} keys; .catch IOException → emit defaults; seen_capture_defaults_dialog_v2; keysFor() helper). Tests: RecordingProfileDefaultTest, RecordingProfileStoreTest, RecordingProfileStoreFallbackTest + FakePreferencesDataStore / ErroringPreferencesDataStore. Folded-in T0: added androidx.datastore:datastore-preferences:1.1.1 + testOptions.unitTests.isReturnDefaultValues=true. Validation: just test passes; openspec validate --strict passes.
close_reason: Phase A ticket 1 implemented per TDD; all tests pass; openspec strict-valid.

title: ScreenTime: ScreenTimeActivity — permission flow and start/stop UI (ticket 8)
description:
Ticket 8 from add-screen-time-monitor-app/tasks.md. Entry point handles all three permissions and lets user enable monitoring.

title: ScreenTime: Foreground service and screen event wiring (ticket 3)
description:
Ticket 3 from add-screen-time-monitor-app/tasks.md. Service stays alive while monitoring and reacts to screen events.

3A Service skeleton:
- 3.1-3.2 ScreenTimeService foreground service with start/stop + persistent notification (instrumentation test).
- 3.3 Register service in AndroidManifest with foregroundServiceType.
3B Screen event receiver:
- 3.4-3.5 ScreenPowerReceiver: SCREEN_ON starts session, SCREEN_OFF (debounced) closes it.
- 3.6-3.7 SCREEN_OFF < 30s does not close; debounce via Handler.postDelayed cancelled on SCREEN_ON.
3C Boot receiver:
- 3.8 ScreenTimeBootReceiver for BOOT_COMPLETED; restart service if monitoring flag persisted in SharedPreferences.
- 3.9 On start, close any open session (null end_time) using last_sample_time or service_start_time; prior-day session closed at 23:59:59.
- 3.10 Register ScreenTimeBootReceiver in AndroidManifest with BOOT_COMPLETED intent filter.
acceptance_criteria: Service starts/stops with persistent notification; screen events drive sessions with debounce; boot recovery and stale-session close work.
notes: Verification: run unit/instrumentation tests via Docker — `just test` (and `just build` for APK). Do NOT run ./gradlew on the host (JDK 25 incompatibility per AGENTS.md).
close_reason: Implemented ScreenTimeService (foreground service + persistent notification + dynamic SCREEN_ON/OFF receiver + Handler 30s debounce + session persistence), ScreenTimeBootReceiver (BOOT_COMPLETED restart gated on prefs flag), ScreenTimeMonitoringPrefs, StaleSessionResolver (pure stale-session close w/ day-clamp). Manifest: service (dataSync FGS) + boot receiver; no new permissions. StaleSessionResolverTest (5 JVM cases) green; just build green. Instrumentation tests for service/receiver/boot added as comments (CI has no emulator job).

title: ScreenTime: Foreground app sampling loop (ticket 4)
description:
Ticket 4 from add-screen-time-monitor-app/tasks.md. Service polls UsageEvents and accumulates per-app intervals.

- 4.1-4.2 Sampling: single app foreground for N samples -> N*5s attributed; 5s poll loop via UsageStatsManager.queryEvents().
- 4.3-4.4 App switch: close correct interval and open new one (event-driven transition).
- 4.5-4.6 Graceful skip when usage access revoked at runtime; null/permission-safe query with notification warning.
- 4.7 Refactor: extract sampling into testable ForegroundAppSampler.
acceptance_criteria: ForegroundAppSampler attributes time correctly across app switches and degrades gracefully when usage access revoked; unit tests pass.
notes: Verification: run unit/instrumentation tests via Docker — `just test` (and `just build` for APK). Do NOT run ./gradlew on the host (JDK 25 incompatibility per AGENTS.md).
close_reason: Implemented ForegroundAppSampler (pure: sample-time accumulation, switch attributed at foreground event time clamped monotonic, system.unattributed fallback, once-only access warning w/ restore) + AndroidForegroundAppSource (UsageStatsManager.queryEvents, reuses UsageAccessChecker, defensive AccessUnavailable). Wired 5s poll loop + usage-access warning notification into ScreenTimeService. ForegroundAppSamplerTest (5 JVM cases incl. N-samples, app switch, revoke/restore) green; just build green.

title: ScreenTime: Checkpoint notification scheduler (ticket 5)
description:
Ticket 5 from add-screen-time-monitor-app/tasks.md. Notifications fire at correct elapsed times within a session.

- 5.1-5.4 Sequence tests: 7 min after start; +6 to reach 13; +16 to reach 29; +29 thereafter.
- 5.5 Implement CheckpointScheduler using Handler.postDelayed.
- 5.6-5.7 Cancel pending callback on session end.
- 5.8-5.9 Resume: read start_time from SessionStore, compute elapsed, derive next checkpoint from [7,13,29,58,87,...] min, schedule remaining delay.
- 5.10 Wire scheduler into ScreenTimeService; post notifications via NotificationManager.
acceptance_criteria: Checkpoints fire at 7/13/29 then every 29 min; cancel-on-end and mid-session resume verified by unit tests.
notes: Verification: run unit/instrumentation tests via Docker — `just test` (and `just build` for APK). Do NOT run ./gradlew on the host (JDK 25 incompatibility per AGENTS.md).
close_reason: Implemented CheckpointSequence (pure 7/13/29-then-29 cadence + next-checkpoint/delay math) and CheckpointScheduler (pure, injectable Timer; start/resume from elapsed, stop/cancel, auto-reschedule). HandlerCheckpointTimer (Handler.postDelayed impl). Wired into ScreenTimeService: start on new session, stop on close (screen-off-within-debounce does NOT reset clock), checkpoint nudge notification on a dedicated channel. CheckpointSchedulerTest (6 JVM cases: cadence, recurring, cancel, resume) green; just build green.

title: SensorLogger: SensorLiveGraphActivity + LiveGraphView (Phase B, ticket 5)
description:
Ticket 5 from update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph/tasks.md. Custom-View line graph showing per-sensor channels in real time, filtered against the session-frozen profile snapshot.

TDD ordering:
- 5.1 RED: LiveGraphViewSnapshotTest (JVM) — setData() with 600-element accel entry → stroke FloatArray of expected length (3 channels × (N-1) × 4 floats).
- 5.2 RED: LiveGraphViewEmptyWindowTest — 0/1 samples → flat placeholder line at band midpoint (no NaN/crash).
- 5.3 RED: Espresso LiveGraphFiltersOnFrozenProfileTest — frozen snapshot says gyro visibleOnGraph=false even if live profile says true → no gyro band.
- 5.4 RED: LiveGraphRotationTest — Activity recreate reads liveStream.value immediately on attach; no blank gap.
- 5.5 GREEN: LiveGraphView extends View; setData() → invalidate().
- 5.6 GREEN: per visible sensor draw one band; multi-axis x/y/z distinct colors; single-value one channel; auto-scaled.
- 5.7 GREEN: empty/singleton handling — flat midpoint line when <2 samples.
- 5.8 GREEN: Activity binds service, combines(liveStream, sessionProfile), filters by sessionProfile[type]?.visibleOnGraph, calls setData() per emission.
- 5.9 GREEN: on Activity onStart, before repeatOnLifecycle collector starts, call setData(liveStream.value filteredBy sessionProfile.value) once for rotation safety.
- 5.10 GREEN: empty states ('Start a recording to see live data' / 'No sensors selected for visualization — open Configure capture').
- 5.11 GREEN: register in AndroidManifest.xml.
- 5.12 REFACTOR: extract drawBand(canvas, band, samples).

Spec: openspec/changes/update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph/tasks.md (Ticket 5)
Code: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/sensorlogger/SensorLiveGraphActivity.kt + LiveGraphView.kt
acceptance_criteria: All Phase 5 tests pass; ≥10 fps under steady recording at SENSOR_DELAY_NORMAL; mid-session visibleOnGraph toggle does NOT hide a band — only next session honors the change.
notes:
SPEC DELTA: openspec/changes/update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph/specs/sensor-logger-ui/spec.md — Requirement ADDED 'Live graph screen'.
CONTINUOUS VALIDATION (DEP-001): On completion, `openspec validate update-sensor-logger-config-and-graph --strict` MUST still pass. Run before marking this ticket done.
AMENDMENT (EXEC-002) — supersedes task 5.1:
5.1 RED (revised): LiveGraphViewSnapshotTest — Given a non-empty sample list for one band, drawing into a test Canvas (or Picture) produces:
  - at least one finite line segment recorded by the canvas
  - every recorded coordinate is finite (no NaN, no Infinity)

title: Epic: usage audit history & drill-down
description:
Extend the usage audit mini-app with daily history browsing and two drill-down levels (Day Detail, App Detail). Users can pick any past day in the platform-retained usage window, see the full ranked app list, hourly distribution, and overnight summary, and drill into a single app's observed foreground intervals.

Spec: openspec/changes/add-usage-audit-history-drilldown/
Builds on: openspec/changes/add-usage-audit-app/ (Today, Last Night, History screens already shipped).

Tracer-bullet plan (after issue-review reslice):
A. Browse a past day's totals — History list + Day Detail (total + ranked apps).
B. Day Detail hourly distribution + scoped Share/CSV.
C. App Detail drill-down (queryEvents adapter + App Detail screen + uninstalled-package state + row-click nav).
D. Tail: update usageaudit/spec/functionality.md and run verification.

Dependency shape: A → B and A → C (B and C parallel after A); D depends on B and C.
notes: Resliced from horizontal (data layer / per-screen / wiring) into vertical tracer bullets following issue review. Old children p5i.1..p5i.5 closed as superseded. New plan: A=Browse-past-day-totals, B=Hourly+Export, C=App-Detail, D=Tail (docs+verify). A is a hard prereq of B and C; B and C are parallel; D depends on both.
close_reason: Epic complete — all 9 slices closed; usage-audit history & drill-down shipped and device-verified

title: Add usageaudit proguard keep rules

title: Scope GitHub Pages deploy to web assets only
description:
deploy.yml uploads the entire repo (path: '.') to GitHub Pages. This includes .wai/, .beads/, android/, openspec/, .git-adjacent files, and other non-web content. The deploy should only include the hub's web assets.

File: .github/workflows/deploy.yml
Fix: either copy web assets to a staging directory and upload that, or switch to a specific path list. Web assets needed: index.html, info.html, style.css, script.js, apps-metadata.json, build-info.json, favicon.png, apple-touch-icon.png, paranoid_logo_bic_pen.svg, and each mini-app's web files.

title: NetDiag: Snapshot Collectors
description:
Implement all network diagnostics collectors and the capture engine.

Tasks:
- 3.1 Create WifiCollector: WifiManager + WifiInfo (API 31+: via NetworkCapabilities.getTransportInfo()) -> WifiSnapshot, scan results -> WifiEnvironment, BSS Load IE parsing (API 30+), graceful degradation on API 26-29. Note: legacy getLinkSpeed() TX value available on all APIs; getTxLinkSpeedMbps()/getRxLinkSpeedMbps() are API 29+ only.
- 3.2 Create CellularCollector: TelephonyCallback (API 31+) / PhoneStateListener -> CellularSnapshot
- 3.3 Create DnsCollector: DnsResolver (API 29+) probes with rcode + latency, fallback to InetAddress for API 26-28, DoH cross-check for hijack detection
- 3.4 Create IpConfigCollector: LinkProperties -> IpConfig with anomaly flags
- 3.5 Create ProbeCollector: icmp4a ping to each ProbeTarget (5 packets, all targets concurrent), OkHttp EventListener HTTP timing to connectivitycheck.gstatic.com
- 3.6 Create DeviceHealthCollector: battery, Doze, Data Saver, airplane mode, VPN, memory, permissions
- 3.7 Create ConnDiagCollector: ConnectivityDiagnosticsManager data (API 30+), null on API 26-29
- 3.8 Create SnapshotCaptureEngine: orchestrate all collectors concurrently via coroutineScope { async {} }, assemble DiagnosticsSnapshot, detect transport changes during capture. Return error (not empty snapshot) when ALL collectors fail.
- 3.9 Write unit tests for each collector's data mapping logic (mock Android APIs where needed)
- 3.10 Write integration test for SnapshotCaptureEngine (instrumentation test on real device)

Spec: openspec/changes/add-netdiag-app/specs/netdiag-snapshot/spec.md
Code: android/app/src/main/kotlin/dev/charly/paranoid/apps/netdiag/
acceptance_criteria: All collectors produce correctly-typed outputs, engine completes within 15s target, unit tests pass, total-failure returns error.
notes: Collectors 3.1-3.7 are independent and can be implemented in any order. 3.8 (engine) depends on all. Write unit tests (3.9) alongside each collector.

title: Derive versionCode/versionName from git tags
description:
android/app/build.gradle.kts hardcodes versionCode=1 and versionName='1.0'. The release workflow creates git tags (v1.0.0, etc.) but the APK always reports version 1.0. The version should be derived from git tags so the installed app reflects the actual release.

File: android/app/build.gradle.kts (lines 14-15)
Fix: add a git-tag-based version derivation in build.gradle.kts (e.g., parse 'git describe --tags' or use a simple tag-to-version function).
close_reason: versionCode/versionName derived from git tags via gradle helper (formula: MAJOR*10000 + MINOR*100 + PATCH; coerced ≥ 1; falls back to 0.0.0/1 if no tag). Verified APK shows versionCode=700/versionName=0.7.0 from v0.7.0. Added git + safe.directory to docker image; fixed broken docker-build recipe.
